fact: February 18 is exactly 49 days into the year - that's 7 weeks of perfect squares!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when maple trees start getting ready to make syrup by moving sugar up from their roots!
February 18 falls smack in the middle of Aquarius season, which means if the zodiac were a pizza, today would be right in the cheesiest part of the water-bearer slice!
Penguins in Antarctica are busy feeding their fluffy gray chicks right now, and the babies are so fat they look like walking cotton balls!
This date sits exactly 32 days before the spring equinox - close enough that you can almost smell warmer weather coming!
In Japan, plum trees are blooming right now and their flowers smell so sweet they make bees dizzy with excitement!
February 18 has 317 days left in the year, which means you've already survived more winter days than you have left!
Arctic foxes are starting to shed their thick white winter coats around this time, leaving tufts of fur everywhere like nature's own cotton candy explosion!
In the Southern Hemisphere, this is peak summer and cicadas are buzzing so loudly they can drown out lawn mowers!
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around February 18 in most places, which means mornings are getting brighter faster than you can say 'pancakes'!
Groundhogs are still deep in hibernation and won't wake up for weeks - they're basically the sleepiest creatures on Earth right now!
In northern regions, this is when ice on lakes gets so thick it sounds like whale songs when it cracks and shifts!
